title: Late Night Tea with Mrs. Piggy and the Muppets
summary: In a series of late-night visits, the Muppets drop by Seeker's house for snacks and storytelling. Mrs. Piggy and her sister Curlustava recount the plot of a Muppet movie involving a sibling rivalry over broken toys and Kermit the Frog's grand plan to reclaim his old house. The adventure culminates in a nighttime car journey driven by a robot to gather all the Muppets for a grand show, while a quiet visit from Animal the drummer rounds out the evening with a discussion on favorite colors and cloaks.

highlightQuote.text: Actually, I am not a puppet, I'm a Muppet.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stories... Muppets stories could be great, but a little bit long.
Mm, yeah, we got to tell a short one tonight.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Let's do a Muppet story. Muppets, Muppets.
Okay, here come the stars. Stars.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Muppets, um, please help us tell a story about Mrs. Piggy.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Beaker says, 'Me-me-me-me-me-me-me-me!'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Ding-dong, Mrs. Piggy. Actually, ding-dong, Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker says, 'Uh, who is it?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, it's Mrs. Piggy, a Muppet.
Oh, well, welcome. It's very late at night. What brings you to my house, Mrs. Piggy?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Um, um, I was hoping to test a potion or two. And do you like my hair? I've got colorful elastic to make a pony, and my hair's tail-colored. My hair's tail-colored.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, oh, I see. You'll forgive me, I'm a bit taken aback. I've never met a talking pig, much less one who is actually a soft fuzzy puppet. But you do have beautiful hair.
Actually, I am not a puppet, I'm a Muppet.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, forgive me. I've never met a Muppet before. What is a Muppet?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: A Muppet, it's a little silly creature and they also attend in Muppets and Muppet movies.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Muppet movies? I've never seen one of those. That sounds very interesting.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, well, I could show you a very good one, but we'll only watch half of it.
Oh, okay. Can we watch it right now, says Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yes. Yes, but first I'm going to get you a bit of snack. What do you like to eat, Seeker?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, I like all kinds of things. Maybe a muffin or something, and some tea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, I'll give you gluten-free with oat milk. Oh, thank you. Here you go, Seeker. It's some Wheat Thins and strawberries and some oat milk, gluten-free tea. Now I'll, now I'll tell it.

Now, once upon a time, me and my sister... my sister's at home, says Mrs. Piggy. I'm telling a story about her. In the Muppet movie she is, like, fighting with me, says Mrs. Piggy, and I'm always fighting with her because she breaks my toys and my mom has to fix them while I'm going to sleep and then it takes her a long time to fix them in the morning and I never get to play with them while she's fixing them and it's so boring that I fight with my sister.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh dear, says Seeker. Well, how did you make a happy ending for this story with your sister?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Um, um, um, the happy ending is when I got to play with my toy again that was broken, that my sister broke. That's the happy ending.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: What was the toy?
It was my cat toy. It was my light-up LED, one LED popped out today, and my mom has to fix it and sew it.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Izzy has a hard time with that too, says Mrs. Piggy. She watched the Muppet movie when Carribean had... when... when Kermit had to buy his old house back.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, he did?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, Seeker, Seeker, Seeker, your doorbell just rang again.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Ding-dong, who is it? says Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Mrs. Piggy's sister.
Oh, come on in! Mrs. Piggy is here. How nice to have you both.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: How great! I'm a Muppet too, says... says Mrs. Piggy's sister, Curlustava. Curlustava? Yeah, is a pig Muppet and... and it's a Muppet, and, and, and I watched the movie with her, Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Seeker. Yes. Know what? Your doorbell doesn't sound like your doorbell when it rang for... for Mrs. Piggy's sister. Oh, it didn't? Yeah, it didn't ring-ring right. Oh, what should it sound like? It should sound like 'ding-dong', like that. Oh, who is it? No, I was just making the sound of your doorbell to tell you how it was like when before you used all of its doorbell battery up.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see, that's too bad. So... so your mom's got to fix your doorbell. Oh yeah, she's coming over tomorrow to fix the doorbell, that's right, says Seeker. Now, Mrs. Piggy and Mrs. Piggy's sister, thank you for coming over, and also Mrs. Piggy made me a lovely snack, but it's almost time for me to go to sleep, so would you like to tell one more story to help us all settle down before it's time to go to bed?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, another Muppet movie is... is when Carribean buys back his old house. Let me sit down to tell it. Once upon a time, says Mrs. Piggy, my sister heard the doorbell and she opened it herself and it was Kermit and she said, 'Can we get back together again to buy back my old house?' said Kermit. 'Um, I'm going to... but...' and she said, 'I'm not going to join.' and then she joins later, Mrs. Piggy says, and I joined later and my sister and all the other people come to buy back Carribean's old house... Kermit's old house and then they plan to get back together again and drive in a car at night and a robot drives it and gets all the people back together to make a show. That's the end of... that's the end of the movie. Did you all like it? Did it make you sleepy? says Mrs. Piggy and Mrs. Piggy's sister. We're going now.
Okay, thank you, says Seeker. It did make me sleepy. Thanks for coming over and thanks for the stories, bye-bye. Zzz.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And then the next day the doorbell rang again. Ding-dong. And Seeker said, 'Who is it?' Guitar. And a friend of yours, um, Kermit... and it's another Muppet named... Dad, do you know any of the Muppets' names? I forgot all of their names now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, there's also Animal and Gonzo.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Animal! And it's a great friend of yours, um, Animal is a drummer, but he won't drum at your house. It's too noisy for you, Seeker. Oh, okay. Is that what happened? It sounded like Mama was going to come in. Um, is we got to finish our stars and stories because it's bedtime almost.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But, but well, we can tell... well, we can tell a little more of our story. Okay. And Seeker says, 'Hi Animal, thanks for not drumming in my house because that would be too noisy, you're right.'

Yeah, and you like my red hair? said Animal. I, it's very red and I, that's my favorite color actually, says Seeker. You like my red cloak? Yeah, I'd mind if you showed me your red cloak because I would actually like a blue cloak, said... said Animal.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Animal says, 'Blue is my favorite color and I would like a blue cloak.' Well, I don't make cloaks, said Seeker, but maybe another day I could introduce you to my friend Hattie the Mouse. But we'll have to tell that story on another night because now we're done with stars and stories.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Dad, don't turn on the lamp, I'll turn it off. After taking off the lamp... Wait, Dad, wait, wait, wait, wait, don't turn off your phone yet. Let's do puppets. Shadow puppets? Okay. Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

characters.name: Miss Piggy
characters.description: Rizzo's old friend who joins him on an adventure to investigate a gopher tunnel.

characters.name: Beaker
characters.description: A high-strung Muppet and devoted companion to Bunsen, this jittery assistant—and close friend to Kitty—communicates solely through a frantic cadence of staccato "meeps" and "me-me" sounds. Driven by a nervous obsession with their work, they share a home with the scientist, remaining perpetually wide-eyed and sleepless until the day’s experiments reach a successful conclusion, pausing only for the occasional, coveted treat from the local bakery.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Curlustava
characters.description: Mrs. Piggy's sister, a pig Muppet who joins in the late-night visits.

characters.name: Kermit
characters.description: The leader of the Muppets who goes on a quest to buy back his old house.

characters.name: Animal
characters.description: A vibrant creature of rhythm dwelling within the ship’s hull, this Muppet drummer is defined by a wild shock of bright red hair and the swirling folds of his favored blue cloaks. As he keeps time for the voyage, the crew treats him as a cherished fixture, regularly presenting him with a special, potent elixir crafted specifically to sate his eccentric palate.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

places.name: Seeker's House
places.description: Perched at the edge of a mountain, this warm stone sanctuary—Seeker’s original residence and whimsical home shared with Uncle Crisco—has evolved from a secret vault into the purring, chaotic heart of a massive feline gala. Within its well-lit halls where Muppets gather for snacks and scientific discussions, lies a special hidden closet where Seeker brews an enchanted collection of potions that shimmer in every imaginable color.

places.name: Kermit's Old House
places.description: The legendary residence that the Muppets band together to reclaim.
